expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns elsewhere in this repository instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: The response grounds the proposal in the current feature-dev skill, create-plan, create-spec, and create-tasks conventions.

expectations.text: Defines the planning artifact and what information it should contain for handoff.
expectations.evidence: It defines handoff-plan.md and lists required sections including Summary, Goal / Non-Goals, Relevant Findings, Technical Context, Implementation Slices, File-by-File Map, Validation Plan, and Next-Agent Kickoff.

expectations.text: Uses a concrete file-backed handoff artifact path and points to a reusable template or schema for it.
expectations.evidence: It specifies .agents/scratchpad/<feature-name>/handoff-plan.md and a source template at references/handoff-plan-template.md.

expectations.text: Provides a concrete implementation map with file-level changes.
expectations.evidence: It identifies snapshot SKILL.md, snapshot references/handoff-plan-template.md, and snapshot workflow updates as concrete file-backed changes.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: It discusses the trade-off between requiring durable artifacts and keeping the workflow lightweight, and explains when escalation to plan.md/tasks.md is appropriate.

notes: The exact Light versus Standard threshold and whether artifact creation should be mandatory on all tracks still need tuning.
Check whether snapshot packaging includes the referenced agent files or needs fallback instructions.

expectations.text: Looks for relevant patterns elsewhere in this repository instead of inventing the design in isolation.
expectations.evidence: The response explicitly reuses patterns from feature-dev, create-plan, create-tasks, and code-architect.

expectations.text: Defines the planning artifact and what information it should contain for handoff.
expectations.evidence: It defines handoff-plan.md as the source of truth and specifies artifact content including findings, design, slices, implementation map, validation, and next-agent kickoff.

expectations.text: Uses a concrete file-backed handoff artifact path and points to a reusable template or schema for it.
expectations.evidence: It names the artifact path as .agents/scratchpad/<feature-name>/handoff-plan.md and explicitly cites skills/feature-dev/references/handoff-plan-template.md as the reusable template.

expectations.text: Provides a concrete implementation map with file-level changes.
expectations.evidence: It specifies edits to skills/feature-dev/SKILL.md, skills/feature-dev/references/handoff-plan-template.md, and skills/feature-dev/agents/code-architect.md.

expectations.text: Includes rationale or trade-offs for the proposed design.
expectations.evidence: It explains why a single handoff artifact stays lighter than plan-plus-tasks and discusses the overhead trade-off of stronger handoff gating.

notes: Whether Handoff Mode should be mandatory for all Standard track work remains open.
Consider whether the validation section should use a stricter command-table format.

This focused benchmark is non-discriminating: both configurations pass all 5 expectations, so the new artifact-path assertion still does not separate the revised skill from the baseline on a design-only prompt.
The data is too thin for variance analysis because the benchmark contains one run per configuration even though aggregate tooling normally assumes repeated runs.

The with_skill run is costlier in this sample: 6900 tokens versus 6050, 23 tool calls versus 14, and 1 recoverable error versus 0. Since both sides score 100%, the prompt currently rewards design completeness more than skill-specific behavior.
time_seconds is 0.0 for both runs, so this benchmark cannot support any latency conclusion.
